The genus Justicia is a part of the family Acanthaceae and consists of around 630 species of flowering plants. intro: Justicia thunbergioides, commonly known as Thunbergia, is a medicinal plant belonging to the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Justicia thunbergioides include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Justicia thunbergioides is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
